name: Google Cloud Platform
description: Public cloud platform; audit evidence via Cloud Audit Logs, IAM policy exports, Security Command Center findings and Cloud Asset Inventory.

L1.title: GCP Cloud Audit Logs Manual Evidence Collection

steps.action: Sign in to the Google Cloud console, select the project or organisation, and navigate to Logging > Logs Explorer
steps.permissions_required: Logs Viewer (roles/logging.viewer); Private Logs Viewer (roles/logging.privateLogViewer) for Data Access audit logs
steps.security_note: Use a dedicated read-only account for evidence collection, not an Owner or Editor identity

steps.action: In the query editor, run logName:"cloudaudit.googleapis.com%2Factivity" for Admin Activity audit logs, or logName:"cloudaudit.googleapis.com%2Fdata_access" for Data Access audit logs, and set the time range to the collection period
steps.audit_note: Admin Activity audit logs are always written and retained for 400 days in the _Required bucket; they cannot be disabled or deleted

steps.action: In the Query results pane, open the Actions menu and click Download, then choose CSV or JSON format
steps.audit_note: Each entry carries a Google-assigned timestamp and insertId, providing tamper-evident ordering

steps.action: For IAM configuration evidence, navigate to IAM & Admin > IAM, or run gcloud projects get-iam-policy PROJECT_ID --format=json and save the output
For security posture evidence, navigate to Security > Security Command Center > Findings, filter by state and category, then use Export > Cloud Storage or Export > CSV
Save all exports with date-stamped filenames including the project ID and upload to the evidence repository
steps.security_note: Review IAM policy exports for service account email addresses before sharing outside the security team; never include service account keys

L2.title: GCP Log Sinks Scheduled Evidence Export

steps.action: Navigate to Logging > Log Router and click Create sink; name the sink, choose a BigQuery dataset or Cloud Storage bucket as the destination
steps.permissions_required: Logs Configuration Writer (roles/logging.configWriter) plus Owner on the destination project

steps.action: Set the inclusion filter to logName:"cloudaudit.googleapis.com" so the sink routes Admin Activity and Data Access audit logs, and grant the sink's writer identity access to the destination
steps.audit_note: Sinks route entries continuously from creation time onward; they are not retroactive, so create them before the audit period begins
steps.security_note: Restrict write access on the destination bucket or dataset to the sink's service account only

steps.action: Enable Data Access audit logs for in-scope services via IAM & Admin > Audit Logs (they are disabled by default except for BigQuery)
steps.audit_note: Without this step, Data Access reads and writes are not logged and the evidence set is incomplete

steps.action: For multi-project estates, create an aggregated sink at the organisation or folder level with --include-children so all project audit logs route to one destination
steps.permissions_required: roles/logging.configWriter on the organisation or folder

steps.action: In Security Command Center, configure a continuous export of findings to BigQuery (Settings > Continuous Exports, or gcloud scc bqexports)
steps.audit_note: Continuous exports write each finding snapshot in near-real time, giving a dated record of posture over time

steps.action: If exporting to Cloud Storage, set a retention policy with Bucket Lock on the evidence bucket to match your retention requirement
steps.audit_note: Bucket Lock makes the retention policy irremovable, demonstrating log immutability to assessors

steps.action: Create a dedicated service account via IAM & Admin > Service Accounts and grant it roles/logging.viewer, roles/logging.privateLogViewer, roles/cloudasset.viewer and roles/securitycenter.findingsViewer
steps.permissions_required: Service Account Admin (roles/iam.serviceAccountAdmin) and Project IAM Admin (roles/resourcemanager.projectIamAdmin)
steps.security_note: Prefer Workload Identity Federation or attached service accounts over downloaded JSON keys; if a key is unavoidable, store it in a secrets manager and rotate it every 90 days
steps.audit_note: Service account creation and key events are themselves recorded in Admin Activity audit logs

steps.action: Schedule a daily job that calls gcloud logging read 'logName:"cloudaudit.googleapis.com" AND timestamp>="START"' --format=json (or the entries.list REST method) and writes the output to the evidence store
steps.permissions_required: roles/logging.viewer (plus roles/logging.privateLogViewer for Data Access logs)

steps.action: Export IAM policy and resource inventory snapshots daily with gcloud asset export --content-type iam-policy --organization ORG_ID --bigquery-table projects/PROJECT_ID/datasets/DATASET/tables/iam_policy --output-bigquery-force (repeat with --content-type resource)
steps.audit_note: Use --snapshot-time to pin each export to a point in time so successive snapshots are directly comparable

steps.action: Pull Security Command Center findings daily with gcloud scc findings list ORGANIZATION_ID --filter='state="ACTIVE"' --format=json and store alongside the log exports
Implement error handling: alert on non-zero exit codes or empty result sets, and re-run failed collections within a defined SLA
steps.audit_note: Keep a run log (start time, end time, record counts) as evidence that the collection process itself is controlled

steps.action: Validate each day's collection against a volume baseline — flag if the audit log entry count drops materially below the trailing average
steps.audit_note: Volume validation provides assurance of collection completeness and surfaces silently broken filters

L4.title: GCP Managed Evidence Pipeline with Completeness Metrics

steps.action: Deploy an organisation-level aggregated log sink routing all Cloud Audit Logs to a partitioned BigQuery dataset (gcloud logging sinks create with --use-partitioned-tables), giving a continuously updated, queryable audit archive
steps.security_note: Lock down the BigQuery dataset with IAM so only the pipeline and auditors can read it

steps.action: Enable Security Command Center continuous exports to BigQuery for findings, and Cloud Asset Inventory feeds to Pub/Sub for real-time notification of resource and IAM policy changes
steps.permissions_required: roles/securitycenter.bigQueryExportsEditor and Cloud Asset Owner (roles/cloudasset.owner) for feeds

steps.action: Track collection completeness with the Cloud Logging exports/error_count and exports/byte_count metrics in Cloud Monitoring, and alert on sink export errors or a sustained drop in routed volume
steps.audit_note: Sink error metrics are the primary evidence that no audit records were dropped in transit

steps.action: Run scheduled BigQuery quality checks: detect gaps in the audit log time series per project, duplicate insertIds, and projects present in Cloud Asset Inventory but absent from the log dataset
steps.audit_note: Cross-referencing asset inventory against log coverage proves every in-scope project is actually being collected

steps.action: Build a Cloud Monitoring or Looker Studio dashboard showing events per day, export error rate, coverage by project, and findings trend from the SCC export
steps.audit_note: The dashboard serves as standing evidence of a managed, monitored collection process

steps.action: Generate an automated weekly collection health report (record counts, gaps detected, SLA breaches, remediation actions) and file it in the evidence repository
steps.audit_note: Periodic health reports demonstrate management review of the evidence pipeline
